# function: cedarvalidate

Returns `true` if `policies` is a syntactically valid [Cedar](https://www.cedarpolicy.com) policy document, `false` otherwise (an empty document is valid). Unlike `cedarformat`, it does not fail the plan on invalid input, so it suits a boolean check in a `precondition` guarding a hand-written `aws_verifiedpermissions_policy` statement.

Validation is syntactic (parsing); it does not check policies against a schema. Backed by [cedar-go](https://github.com/cedar-policy/cedar-go), the official Go implementation of Cedar.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// cedarvalidate: is a string a syntactically valid Cedar policy document?
// Returns a bool and never fails the plan, so it suits a precondition.
output "valid" {
  value = provider::burnham::cedarvalidate("permit (principal, action, resource) when { resource.owner == principal };")
  // → true
}

output "invalid" {
  value = provider::burnham::cedarvalidate("permit (principal, action, resource")
  // → false
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
cedarvalidate(policies string) bool
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `policies` (String) A Cedar policy document to check.
